World Cup Gameweek 1

Guadalajara Stadium

# Team News: Team News: South Korea vs Czech Republic injury, suspension list, predicted XIs

By Jonathan O'Shea| 10 Jun 2026 03:00

© Imago

Getting under way in Guadalajara, both South Korea and the Czech Republic will commence their Group A campaign at World Cup 2026 when they meet on Thursday.

While the Taegeuk Warriors strolled through Asian qualifying their Czech counterparts came through two UEFA playoff rounds on penalties to make it to North America.

Here, Sports Mole rounds up the latest team news for both sides.

---

## SOUTH KOREA vs. CZECH REPUBLIC

© Imago

## SOUTH KOREA

Out: None

Doubtful: None

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Kim Seung-gyu; Lee Gi-hyuk, Kim Min-jae, Lee Han-beom; Seol Young-woo, Hwang In-beom, Paik Seung-ho, Lee Tae-seok; Hwang Hee-chan, Lee Jae-sung; Son Heung-min

## CZECH REPUBLIC

O

# "Czech Match Best 11 Confirmed" Coach Hong Myung-bo Announces Directly "I Was Worried Until Morning..." [World Cup D-1 On-site Q&A]

Published:2026.06.11 ・ 06:12

Updated:2026.06.11 ・ 13:10

Park Geondo

Add as preferred source

*This content was translated by AI.

The South Korean national team held a press conference on the 10th at Estadio Akron in Guadalajara, Mexico, one day before the 2026 CONCACAF World Cup Group A qualifier against Czechia. Coach Hong Myung-bo is announcing his lineup. /Photo=Kim Jin-kyung kim.jinkyung@

Coach Hong Myung-bo of the South Korean national football team, aiming for redemption in his second World Cup, issued a confident statement one day before the opening match of the tournament.

Coach Hong Myung-bo's Lee Kkeu-neun (CEO) team will face Czechia in the first match of the 2026 FIFA World Cup in North America and the Caribbean Group A at Estadio Akron in Guadalajara on the morning of the 12th (Korean time).

At the official press conference held at the decisive venue, Estadio Akron, at 2:30 p.m. local tim

news_items.title: South Korea vs Czechia in World Cup 2026: Kick-off time, how to watch, stats, team news - ESPN
news_items.author: Sunaadh Sagar
news_items.text:
South Korea vs Czechia in World Cup 2026: Kick-off time, how to watch, stats, team news - ESPN

Skip to main content Skip to navigation

play

South Korea and Czechia begin their 2026 FIFA World Cup campaign in Guadalajara, Mexico on Thursday at Estadio Chivas in a Group A encounter.

Czechia are making their first appearance in the World Cup since 2006, after riding their luck through the qualifying campaign. In contrast, Hong Myung-bo's side went unbeaten en-route to qualification. Neither side are considered among the tournament's favourites, although a relatively easy group that also features co-hosts Mexico and South Africa ought to raise hopes for a knockout appearance.

The two teams have faced each other in three friendlies before, with a win each.

Here's everything you need to know about the second match of the tournament.

## How to watch:

The match will be available on ITV1 in the UK, Fox Sports in the U.S., Zee5 in India and SBS in Australia. You can also follow ESPN's live updates.

## Key Details:

Date, kick-off time: Thursday, June 11, 8 p.m. Guadalajara (10 p.m. EDT; 3 a.m. BST, 7.30 a.m. IST, Friday and 12 p.m. AEST, Friday) Venue: Estadio Chivas, Guadalajara, M

[Sapo Pan (Mexico) = Newsis] An Gyeongnam reporter = Hong Myung-bo's injury has occurred ahead of the first group stage of the 2026 North American World Cup against the Czech Republic.

Hong Myung-bo made the final quenching in preparation for the first Group A match against the Czech Republic at the Chivas Baye Verde training site in Sapopan, near Guadalajara, Mexico on the 11th (Korea time).

Two players were missing from the last training session.

Center back Kim Tae-hyun (Kashima Antlers) was sidelined following striker Bae Joon-ho (Stoke City), who has been training separately since entering Mexico.

An official from the national team said, "Kim Tae-hyun fell and injured his ankle

South Korea and Czechia begin their 2026 FIFA World Cup campaign in Guadalajara, Mexico on Thursday at Estadio Chivas in a Group A encounter.

Czechia are making their first appearance in the World Cup since 2006, after riding their luck through the qualifying campaign. In contrast, Hong Myung-bo's side went unbeaten en-route to qualification. Neither side are considered among the tournament's favourites, although a relatively easy group that also features co-hosts Mexico and South Africa ought to raise hopes for a knockout appearance.

The two teams have faced each other in three friendlies before, with a win each.

Here's everything you need to know about the second match of the tournament.

## How to watch:

The match will be available on ITV1 in the UK, Fox Sports in the U.S., Zee5 in India and SBS in Australia. You can also follow ESPN's live updates.

## Key Details:
